_meta.method: Web research across Helsinki Zen Center (helsinki.zazen.fi), Sanneji Zen Temple, Tampere Zen Center, Sydänmieli Zen, Buddhalainen Liitto references, Ordinary Mind Zendo Europe
_meta.coverageEstimate: ~85% of active organized Zen groups in Finland
knownGaps: Helsinki Zenshala (Plum Village) referenced in prompt - 'Zenshala' did not appear in searches; no Finnish Plum Village sangha located online with that name
Kwan Um School: no Finnish center listed in Kwan Um Europe directory
Sanbo Zen Suomi: not located as a distinct Finnish entity
Soto Zen Suomi (sotozen.fi) referenced in prompt - domain not found via search results; possibly inactive
Kuopio zen group reportedly led by Sensei Mitra Virtaperko but no standalone web presence located

places.name: Helsinki Zen Center
places.notes: Weekly zazen in Helsinki since 1997; teacher Sangen Salo Sensei (resides at Sanneji); Yasutani-Kapleau line via Zenbuddhistiska Samfundet network

places.name: Sanneji Zen Temple
places.notes: Residential Zen temple in Karjaa; Sangen Salo Sensei resides here; full-time practice and sesshin retreats; maintained by Sanneji Zen ry

places.name: Sanneji Zendo Turku
places.notes: Affiliated zendo of Sanneji / Helsinki Zen Center network

places.name: Tampere Zen Center
places.notes: Tampere Zen Center ry, registered association founded 2007; ~30 members (2024); led by Sensei Mitra Virtaperko (sensei since 2021 in Yasutani-Kapleau line); zazen 3-4x/week

places.name: Sydänmieli Zen Helsinki
places.notes: Connected to Tampere Zen Center; led by Sensei Mitra Virtaperko; regular meditation, weekend retreats, sesshin
